description: When prediction recall is persistently low (<=0.5) while the dependency graph looks correct, suspect cascade direction bug in generate-predictions.js::buildAdjacency. Demonstrated R14: adj[to].add(from) made BFS find upstream DEPENDENCIES instead of downstream CONSUMERS — editing X predicted files X reads from, not files that read X. Fixed by inverting to adj[from].add(to). Pattern: low recall + known shifts = suspect cascade direction.

trigger.check: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('metrics/hme-prediction-accuracy.json')); rds=[r for r in d.get('rounds',[]) if not r.get('skipped') and r.get('recall') is not None][-3:]; print('recall low:'+str([r.get('recall') for r in rds])) if len(rds)==3 and all(r.get('recall',1) <= 0.5 for r in rds) else None"

action.summary: Diagnose whether the adjacency direction is wrong.
steps: Identify one confirmed-edited src/ file from last round (via git diff HEAD~2..HEAD).
Run generate-predictions.js for that file with MAX_DEPTH=1; check the predicted affected_modules list.
Manually verify: are the predicted modules ones that READ from the edited file (correct) or ones the edited file READS FROM (inverted)?
If inverted: flip adj[to].add(from) to adj[from].add(to). Add a validate_adjacency test case with a known file pair.
Verify recall recovers to 1.0 next pipeline run.
